properties.name: City Centre & Baltic Triangle
properties.verdict: If you want to walk everywhere and never need a car, this is it. Baltic Triangle is the creative/nightlife hub. You pay for the convenience.
notes: Lively at night — noisy on weekends

properties.name: Business District & Waterfront
properties.verdict: Dale Street, the waterfront, Moorfields. Quieter than L1 at night, great for Pier Head walks.
notes: Offices by day, calm by night
Very few supermarkets — walk to L1

properties.name: Vauxhall & North Docks
properties.verdict: Cheap and close to town, but it's one of the most deprived wards in England. Visit before you commit.
notes: New developments creeping north from the centre

properties.name: Anfield & Walton
properties.verdict: Home of Liverpool FC — buzzing on matchdays, rough around the edges otherwise. Very cheap, but do your homework street by street.
notes: Some streets fine, some not — varies block to block

properties.name: Everton
properties.verdict: Next to the new Everton stadium at Bramley-Moore Dock, so it's changing fast. Right now: cheap, rough, up-and-coming.
notes: Stadium regeneration may lift the area

properties.name: Kensington & Fairfield
properties.verdict: Big student population near the universities. Cheap rooms, mixed streets, standard big-city caution applies.

properties.name: Edge Hill & Knowledge Quarter
properties.verdict: Student central — unis, hospitals, cheap eats. Fine if you're young and don't mind hustle.
notes: Walkable to the centre and both unis

properties.name: Toxteth, Dingle & Princes Park
properties.verdict: Has a rep from the 80s, but Princes Park side is genuinely nice now — creatives, families, Lark Lane round the corner. Dingle side is rougher. Street-by-street call.

properties.name: Walton, Aintree & Fazakerley
properties.verdict: Proper residential north Liverpool. Aintree has the racecourse and good rail links. Unflashy, decent value.

properties.name: Norris Green & Clubmoor
properties.verdict: Big 1930s council estate, one of the poorest areas in the city. Cheap rents reflect that.

properties.name: West Derby & Croxteth
properties.verdict: West Derby village is leafy and old-school nice; Croxteth is rougher. Same postcode, different worlds — check which end.
notes: West Derby village: pubs, greens, families

properties.name: Old Swan & Tuebrook
properties.verdict: Solid working-class area, good value, decent shops on Prescot Road. Nothing fancy, nothing scary.

properties.name: Broadgreen & Dovecot
properties.verdict: Quiet residential, Edge Lane retail park nearby. Boring in a good way.

properties.name: Wavertree
properties.verdict: The classic student/young-professional area — Smithdown Road is legendary. Great value, great bus links, always something going on.
notes: Very popular with students & first renters

properties.name: Childwall
properties.verdict: Leafy suburb, good schools, families. Quiet and safe, slightly dull if you're 25.

properties.name: Aigburth & Sefton Park
properties.verdict: Arguably the best quality-of-life postcode for a newcomer: Sefton Park, Lark Lane, the river promenade. Green, fun, safe-ish, well connected.
notes: Train from St Michaels → town in 8 mins

properties.name: Allerton & Mossley Hill
properties.verdict: Posh-ish suburbia. Allerton Road has proper restaurants and delis. Safe, green, family-friendly, slightly pricey.
notes: Allerton Rd high street is great

properties.name: Garston & Speke
properties.verdict: South end of the city near the airport. Mixed — Garston village is fine, parts of Speke are rough. Good value if you pick well.
notes: New retail park at New Mersey

properties.name: Woolton & Gateacre
properties.verdict: Village-feel suburbia, very pretty, very safe. Woolton village has pubs and a cinema. Feels miles from the city (in a good way).

properties.name: Bootle
properties.verdict: Cheapest rents near the city for a reason — high deprivation and crime stats. Well connected by train, but research carefully.

properties.name: Litherland & Seaforth
properties.verdict: Similar story to Bootle — cheap, deprived, but on the train line. Some pockets by the water are improving.
notes: Train line is the main asset

properties.name: Waterloo & Crosby
properties.verdict: Seaside suburbia — Waterloo has a great little high street and the beach with the Gormley statues. Underrated gem.
notes: Another Place iron men on the beach

properties.name: Crosby & Blundellsands
properties.verdict: Leafy, beachy, posh. Blundellsands is one of the nicest suburbs in Merseyside. If you want calm and sea air, here.

properties.name: Kirkby
properties.verdict: Separate town north-east of Liverpool. Very cheap, but limited amenities and higher crime stats. Know what you're signing up for.

properties.name: Huyton & Roby
properties.verdict: Mixed estate-and-suburb area east of the city. Roby side is nicer, has a station with direct trains to Manchester too.

properties.name: Birkenhead Centre
properties.verdict: Cheapest way to live 10 minutes from Liverpool city centre — but the town centre is struggling and crime stats are high. Trade-off postcode.
notes: Hamilton Square station → town in 4 mins

properties.name: Tranmere & Rock Ferry
properties.verdict: Tranmere Rovers territory. Very cheap, rough in parts. The river views from the promenade are genuinely stunning though.
notes: Ferry views of the Liverpool skyline

properties.name: Prenton & Oxton
properties.verdict: Oxton village is a hidden gem — conservation area, arty vibe, lovely pubs. Best value 'nice area' on the Wirral side.

properties.name: Wallasey & Liscard
properties.verdict: Working-class seaside town feel. Mixed — some nice streets near the prom, some rough ones inland. Cheap with character.

properties.name: New Brighton
properties.verdict: Seaside resort having a genuine revival — marine lake, indie bars, beach, Fort Perch Rock. Fun place to live if you don't mind tourists in summer.

properties.name: Moreton & Meols
properties.verdict: Quiet residential north Wirral coast. Meols is nicer and near the sea; Moreton is more basic. Safe and sleepy.

properties.name: Hoylake
properties.verdict: Posh seaside village — Royal Liverpool Golf Club, beach, nice restaurants. Lovely if you can handle the commute.

properties.name: West Kirby & Caldy
properties.verdict: The Wirral's crown jewel — marine lake, Hilbre Island walks, great schools, proper restaurants. Premium prices, premium life.
notes: Walk to Hilbre Island at low tide

properties.name: Upton & Greasby
properties.verdict: Solid middle-Wirral suburbia. Safe, family-friendly, well connected, zero excitement. Great if that's what you want.

properties.name: Heswall
properties.verdict: Affluent, pretty, Dee estuary sunsets, bars and restaurants on the hill. The poshest postcode on the Wirral — priced accordingly.
notes: Buzzing high street for a small town

properties.name: Pensby & Irby
properties.verdict: Quiet family suburbs between Heswall and the motorway. Safe, green, handy for both coast and city.

properties.name: Bromborough & Eastham
properties.verdict: Eastham has the country park and village charm; Bromborough is more industrial/retail. Decent commuter value.
notes: Eastham Country Park on the Mersey

properties.name: Bebington & Higher Bebington
properties.verdict: Port Sunlight village (CH62 edge) is tourist-brochure beautiful, and Bebington proper is solid suburbia. Underrated and well priced.
notes: Port Sunlight: model village & Lady Lever gallery

properties.name: Neston & Parkgate
properties.verdict: Little market town on the Dee estuary — Parkgate's marshes and ice cream are famous. Feels like Cheshire, because it nearly is.
